content:
ok, now the envirment is ok.
/linux-rust# make LLVM=1 rustavailable
Rust is available!
but 'Rust support' doen't appear in General setup menu, should I set CONFIG_RUST=y in .config ?

Hello,
To enable rust you must run make LLVM=1 menuconfig (or your prefered configuration tool), don't forget the LLVM=1
If you can't see it, just search for the symbol CONFIG_RUST, it will shows the dependencies needed for rust, maybe you enabled one of them

For example you can see here that by default MODVERSIONS is enabled and RUST can only be active if MODVERSION is disabled.

If you want to activate the minimum set of features to run the kernel in qemu and use Rust, you can run make LLVM=1 allnoconfig qemu-busybox-min.config rust.config(taken from https://www.jackos.io/rust-kernel/rust-for-linux.html#dependencies)

Louis Chauvet said:

To enable rust you must run make LLVM=1 menuconfig (or your prefered configuration tool), don't forget the LLVM=1
If you can't see it, just search for the symbol CONFIG_RUST, it will shows the dependencies needed for rust, maybe you enabled one of them

For example you can see here that by default MODVERSIONS is enabled and RUST can only be active if MODVERSION is disabled.

If you want to activate the minimum set of features to run the kernel in qemu and use Rust, you can run make LLVM=1 allnoconfig qemu-busybox-min.config rust.config(taken from https://www.jackos.io/rust-kernel/rust-for-linux.html#dependencies)

thank you, it works now. by the way, the picture you post which show the dependency of CONIFG is which command?

Simply make LLVM=1 menuconfig, then press / and type the symbol you want to search
If you want to access to the symbol whithout search it in the hierarchy, you can press the number shown with parenthesis (1) on my screenshoft for example.

If you want to access to the symbol whithout search it in the hierarchy, you can press the number shown with parenthesis (1) on my screenshoft for example.

Have you tried to just do make at the root ? It may be that you didn't build the bindings between rust and c

Louis Chauvet said:

Have you tried to just do make at the root ? It may be that you didn't build the bindings between rust and c

yes, i performed 'make LLVM=1' at root , its building is ok and generated some *.ko in /samples/rust/
because i fcous on the rust programming, so I enter the direcotry /samples/rust to build rust code seperatly

I only build the whole project, so I don't know, sorry :sweat_smile:
I think I only loose 1 or 2 seconds before it compiles the samples

because i fcous on the rust programming, so I enter the direcotry /samples/rust to build rust code seperatly

The build system will only build what changed, so typically there is no need for that if you want to build all Rust samples.

Please avoid M= -- it is only meant for external modules. The other one, -C, should be fine (it is just a Make flag). In my case, I use a dedicated terminal for building that I keep in the root instead.

Miguel Ojeda said:

because i fcous on the rust programming, so I enter the direcotry /samples/rust to build rust code seperatly

The build system will only build what changed, so typically there is no need for that if you want to build all Rust samples.

Please avoid M= -- it is only meant for external modules. The other one, -C, should be fine (it is just a Make flag). In my case, I use a dedicated terminal for building that I keep in the root instead.

thanks for reply, i tried again and found that the command 'make' should be executed at the directory of root and can not be excuted at the direcotry of 'samples/rust' even if I try to only make the code in 'samples/rust'
You're welcome! Yes, that is expected. You can use -C to change the folder if you really want to run it in samples/rust, but please avoid M=.

Miguel Ojeda said:

You're welcome! Yes, that is expected. You can use -C to change the folder if you really want to run it in samples/rust, but please avoid M=.

i see. by the way, can i change the kernel of ubuntu 22.04 from '5.15.0-67-generic' to '6.2'? i make this project in ubuntu 22.04, then make modules_install and make install, after i reboot , the system crashed

It should be possible, but I suggest using a virtual machine with a minimal system instead (see this LF Live: Mentorship Series video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-l-8WrGHEGI for a hands-on walkthrough of that).

Miguel Ojeda said:

It should be possible, but I suggest using a virtual machine with a minimal system instead (see this LF Live: Mentorship Series video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-l-8WrGHEGI for a hands-on walkthrough of that).

well, at first i use make LLVM=1 to compile kernel and upgraded, but system crashed. then I use make (means using gcc), upgraded successfully. i dont know why. :sweat_smile:
